title: Using a single mirror column to pull information from multiple boards - Multi Board Mirror
excerpt:
Hi everyone - my apologies if this particular use case was discussed in a different thread. 
Is there a way to use a single mirror column to connect each item (row/pulses) to a different board instead of creating a diffe…

posts.author: nguz (Nelson G.)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone - my apologies if this particular use case was discussed in a different thread.

Is there a way to use a single mirror column to connect each item (row/pulses) to a different board instead of creating a different column for each connected board?

I am currently managing multiple regional projects, and each requires its own board structure due to its complexity. For this reason, I cannot have all projects on the same low-level board and use groups to organize them (which would make my use case a lot simpler).

I wish to use my Regional High-Level board to view all regional projects in one place (like a Summary Overview). Each item in this board will represent a different project. Therefore, I want to connect each item to its Project High-Level board to get information automatically, like timeline, status, etc.

The reason why I’m not looking into dashboards for this, it’s because I need the Regional Board to be dynamic for data updates like budget, prioritization, etc.

Here’s an example of my structure:

→ Regional High Level board (regional summary)
→ Project High Level board (project summary)
–>Project Low Level board (project tasks)

image931×232 18.4 KB

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @nguz

It looks like the app “Rollup Multiple Boards” is a possible solution for this. You can read more about this app (currently in review for the marketplace) at 20|Rollup Multiple Boards - Excellent Team

posts.author: AronK (Aron-monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @nguz!

We’ve recently rolled out our new multi-board mirror feature which should solve this use case for you

Here is what it looks like: Multi-Board Mirror

Does this fit your use case? If so, please provide me with your account URL so that I can add this feature to your account in case it is not already added.

-Aron

posts.author: rafael.lainez.dalog (Rafael)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear Aron,

This function sounds exactly what I need. I am currently doing some testing to present to my company for the approval of the platform.

We have several projects (Boards) each board has 5 groups (each group has several items and some of this items have subitems).
We will have maybe 100 boards or so.

I want to have the status of all this 100 boards in one central High level dashbaord with the “multi-board Mirror” that you spoke. This solution from the video you posted will give a quick view of the status of each project very summarized and quick.

The plan then after having this High level board to connect it to a dashbaord to see even more nice for projects that have been updated, etc…

But important for now for me to present is how can I get this feature of the Multi-board Mirror? I do not see it possible on my trial account.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @rafael.lainez.dalo and welcome to the community, The multi-board mirroring is wonderful for a quick snapshot of high-level data as you mention, however, reporting off of this data into a dashboard is not overly friendly. This is due to the lack of support of mirrored columns on dashboard widgets.

You may want to look into building a master/child board approach where the child (project) boards are pulling from the master board. This in turn gives you a single board to report from and does not have any of the dashboard limitations (10 boards with Pro, 50 with enterprise). As you mention, if you have 100+ boards to report from - this may be a perfect fit.

If you would like some guidance on how to set this up feel free to DM me here or shoot me a message info@greenllamas.com.

Also feel free to check us out online to learn more about what we do!

Thanks,
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: jvandoorn (Jackie van Doorn)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @AronK - I just wanted to let you know that I’m so excited about this feature, but it doesn’t seem to be working so well. When I’ve added a new connect boards column and asked to to create one on the other boards as well - it seems to selectively choose which connect boards will mirror onto the high-level board. I thought maybe I just needed to change the order I connected them, so I deleted all the connected columns and started over, linking from the high-level to the low-level boards and that worked! But then - the connections all disappeared! I’m guessing it’s just some glitch on monday’s end, but would LOVE to use this feature - I have a real need for it for a project I’m currently deep into, so would love to see if this kink can be worked out. Thanks so much!

posts.author: AronK (Aron-monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! Apologies to hear you are running into issues with this. As you are experiencing buggy issues with this, it would be best to send an email to our support team so that they can troubleshoot further with you

posts.author: jvandoorn (Jackie van Doorn)
posts.cookedHtml: @AronK got it, thank you!

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @jvandoorn - There was an interim version of multi-linking that was given to some account that didn’t fully support the multi-mirror capability. In that case, what would happen is the first board you connected when linking boards would be the only one that would mirror. I suspect you have that version. Support can check and update you to the full multi-link + multi-mirror version that is available now.

posts.author: jvandoorn (Jackie van Doorn)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @PolishedGeek! Thanks for your reply. That could be the case, but SOMETIMES it connects multiple boards, and sometimes it doesn’t. To get any functioning connections, I have to refresh after every connection on both boards, and still that is not 100% foolproof. It doesn’t even seem to connect at all: the “connect” column won’t even show the items that I’ve linked.

posts.author: maryt (Mary)
posts.cookedHtml:
@AronK - This feature seemed perfect for our needs. However, it doesn’t work out very well. We are facing a lot of mirroring issues ever since this has been activated. Like some users could see some of the mirrored columns and some do not.

I thought duplicating all the values in the column and deleting the older column would help in getting everything back to normal. However, it created a new board and I had to disconnect the board. The remnants can still be seen; due to this feature enabled, it has created two boards. Please note the duplicate board does not exist, I had just duplicated the mirrored column.

Could you please deactivate yourfilm-production.monday.com from this feature to avoid our main operations from going haywire? Thanks in advance.

posts.author: AronK (Aron-monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: @maryt apologies to hear you were running into issues here. As this is still not rolled out 100%, I have removed it from your account per your request. With regard to not hearing back from support, I would like to take a look and see if we did indeed receive a ticket from you to see why you have not heard back yet. Can you share the email address you sent this from? If this is something our team is able to troubleshoot and fix for you, we can always add the feature back.

posts.author: maryt (Mary)
posts.cookedHtml:
@AronK - Thank you for removing us from this feature. It helped removed some of the issues we were facing. We are gonna analyse this for the next couple of days and see how it goes and if we’re still facing any mirroring issues. We did hear back from the team and they did say that the issues we were facing were quite odd. However, after giving it some time, some users were able to view the mirrored column correctly and some did not.

I think we’re happy at the moment without this feature. It has caused us 3 issues so far affecting our operations. Thank you for your help.

posts.author: AliKarout (Ali Karout)
posts.cookedHtml:
What if one of the Project have over 100 item, is there a way faster than having to select each item in the connect boards cell? In other word, let take Project 1 for example and pretend there is 100 items on Project one instead. Is there anyway to tell the cell Connect Board to select all item so the status of Project 1 show a summary of the whole Project 1 board.

Thank a lot for answering

posts.author: AronK (Aron-monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @AliKarout! At the moment, the best way to save time with so many items to connect would be to batch-action select the 100 items in the project and then connect the batch of 100 items to the project items it aligns with on the other board. Once this is done, those 100 items should be connected on the high level board so that you do not need to select them one by one on that board. Take a look here > example for shortcut and let me know if you run into any issues replicating on your end
